description: Content-aware intra-cluster premise ranking, the lever a-0014 named as 'remaining'. SAME bridge routing (home + top-5 bridge-neighbour clusters), same candidate accounting, same 432 held-out cross-domain TEST theorems as e-0015. Only the intra-cluster ordering changes: rank candidate declarations by IDF-weighted lexical overlap between the QUERY theorem's name tokens and each declaration's name tokens (full_name split on '.', '_', camelCase, digits; IDF over all clustered decls; popularity tiebreak). The only leakage-free content signal in the corpus (no type text exists) and one the prover always has (the goal theorem's own name).
motivation: a-0014 found popularity ranking cracks moderate recall but collapses on the cross-domain tail (0.90 recall still needs ~90% of the pool) because the last ~20% of cross-domain premises are rarely-cited specialized lemmas a popularity prior cannot surface. Test whether a content-aware ranker reaches that tail cheaply.
formal_results: 432 cross-domain test theorems, ceiling recall 0.9341 at mean pool 53.8k (identical to e-0015). Budget-to-reach: content vs popularity vs random -- recall>=0.70: 7.2k vs 12.3k vs 40.1k; >=0.80: 11.7k vs 26.8k vs 45.9k; >=0.90: 19.5k vs 47.9k vs 51.8k candidates. Content dominates popularity at every budget (e.g. 0.906 recall at ~19.9k vs popularity 0.772). Diagnostic at B=2000: content surfaces 1,078 premises popularity misses, median train-citation count = 2.
results_description: Content ranking reaches 0.90 cross-domain premise recall at ~19.5k candidates (36% of the 53.8k pool) vs popularity's ~47.9k (89%): a 2.46x cost cut exactly where a-0014 reported popularity collapsed to 1.07x over random. The 1,078 tail premises content recovers that popularity misses are rarely-cited (median 2 train citations) -- the specialized cross-domain lemmas a-0014 said a popularity prior could not reach.
conclusions: The content-aware intra-cluster ranker a-0014 named IS the cheap tail lever. IDF-weighted query-name token overlap surfaces the rarely-cited specialized cross-domain premises that popularity cannot, cutting the cost of 0.90 cross-domain premise recall 2.46x and making EXHAUSTIVE cross-domain retrieval -- not just moderate recall -- substantially cheaper under bridge routing. Still a retrieval proxy, not a proving run: q-0011's operational prover-success payoff remains unmeasured, but the remaining retrieval lever is now demonstrated to work.
